back_translation: The LORD appointed a great fish to swallow Jonah, and Jonah was in the belly of that fish for three days and three nights.
Jonah prayed to the LORD his God from the belly of the fish.
He said: "In my distress I called out to the LORD, and he answered me. From the belly of the realm of the dead I cried for help, and you heard my voice.
You hurled me down into the deep, into the heart of the sea, and the current surrounded me; all your breakers and waves swept over me.
I had said, 'I have been driven out from before your sight, but I will yet look upon your holy temple again.'
Waters surrounded me up to my neck; the deep encircled me; seaweed wrapped around my head.
I went down to the roots of the mountains; the earth barred me in with its bolts forever — but you brought up my life from the pit, O LORD my God!
When my soul fainted within me, I remembered the LORD, and my prayer went up to you, to your holy temple.
Those who cling to worthless idols forsake the steadfast love they could have received.
But as for me, with a voice of thanksgiving I will offer sacrifice to you. What I have vowed, I will fulfill. Salvation belongs to the LORD!"
Then the LORD spoke to the fish, and it vomited Jonah out onto dry land.
